description:
# Open question

Standardize how balls and its plugins emit observability — logs and metrics — instead of the ad-hoc conventions accreting per phase. bl-5d56 (§6 dispatch) invented `clones/<enc>/logs/<name>/<op>-<phase>.log` (append) with zero spec backing; §6 only says "stderr: human log; captured to clones/<enc>/logs/<name>/" and §1 only reserves `clones/<enc>/logs/`. There is no metrics concept anywhere. Two ad-hoc choices already exist; a third will diverge.

## Subtraction-first position (argue me UP from here)

- METRICS ARE NOT CORE — probably not anywhere yet. balls is a local git-native tracker; the git log already records every op (§5 trailers: op/actor/id/commit). "Metrics" over that is a QUERY, not a stored stream. If timing/counters are ever wanted, they are a `*.post` plugin that watches the op lifecycle and writes wherever it likes — core emits none. Default: cut "metrics" from scope until a concrete consumer exists.
- LOGS ARE A THIN DERIVED CONVENTION, NOT CONFIG. Core owns exactly one fact: WHERE a plugin's stderr lands (`logs/<name>/...`), a DERIVED path (encode of name + op/phase), never a configured one — single-source-of-truth, severable. No rotation/retention/levels in core: a log file is append-only stderr; the human (or doctor/prime) prunes, same stale-but-harmless rule as orphan worktrees.

## Concrete decisions to nail

1. PLUGIN LOG PATH — exact layout + filename. bl-5d56 picked `logs/<name>/<op>-<phase>.log`; ratify or change. Per-op-phase file vs per-run vs one appended stream? Does `doctor` (bl-e8a5) audit/prune it?
2. BALLS' OWN DIAGNOSTICS — core writes to its own stderr today (message.rs/git.rs errors). Captured anywhere, or just inherited to the user's terminal? Should core op-level diagnostics share the `logs/` tree or stay on stderr?
3. THE PLUGIN-FACING CONTRACT — today a plugin just writes stderr and balls routes it (no env telling it where). Keep that (balls owns routing, plugin stays dumb), or expose a `BALLS_LOG_DIR` for structured artifacts beyond stderr? Lean: keep dumb — stderr IS the interface, no new env (a new env/flag is a smell).
4. METRICS, IF IT SURVIVES THE CUT — define the minimal seam (a lifecycle event a plugin can subscribe to) and confirm it is plugin-only, no core storage.

## Resolution folds into

The §1 layout + §6 dispatch sections of spec bl-2e26 (ratify the `logs/` layout there) and retro-confirms or amends bl-5d56's invented path. Relates to doctor (bl-e8a5) for any prune/audit duty.
title: Topic: standardize observability interfaces — logs and metrics
